title: Revert product table RLS to project_isolation

hypotheses.claim: Migration SQL correctly drops user_project_access and restores project_isolation on all 5 product tables
hypotheses.prediction: All 5 tables have project_isolation policy; user_can_access_project() function is gone

hypotheses.claim: Restored project_isolation policies match MCP server's JWT minting (project claim in JWT)
hypotheses.prediction: JWT with { project: 'demo' } enables CRUD; different project returns 0 rows
hypotheses.validation: Integration test with setProject() against live Supabase

hypotheses.claim: service_role_bypass policies remain functional alongside restored project_isolation
hypotheses.prediction: Service role sees all rows regardless of project; authenticated client sees only its project
hypotheses.validation: Query with both service_role and authenticated clients

hypotheses.claim: 33 integration tests pass after the revert migration is applied
hypotheses.prediction: pnpm test -- --grep supabase shows 33 passing tests

hypotheses.claim: Cross-project isolation holds — two projects see zero overlap
hypotheses.prediction: Two storage instances with different projects see only their own rows; cross-project INSERT rejected by WITH CHECK
hypotheses.validation: Integration test with two SupabaseStorage instances scoped to different projects

open_risks: No workspace auto-provisioning timeline — interim solution has no defined end
SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET correctness on Cloud Run unverified
auth.jwt() requires Postgres 13+ / standard Supabase tier
Service role key must never be exposed to clients
CREATE POLICY not idempotent (documented workaround exists)

reconciliation_flags.evidence: ADR assumes workspace memberships are auto-populated on signup. No auto-provisioning code exists.
AUTH-01 rejected custom JWT middleware. Revert temporarily restores it as interim.
